Strategy Name: Market Sentiment and an Overnight Anomaly
Category: Sentiment Trading, Market Timing, Alternative Data
Strategy Description: An overnight trading strategy for SPY ETF that uses market sentiment and trend indicators to time entries.
Core Logic: The US equity premium is largely earned overnight. High opening prices often decline in the first hour. Sentiment filters (VIX, BMS, Trend) identify high-probability overnight sessions.
Entry Conditions: Buy SPY ETF 16 minutes before close and hold overnight if: SPY > 20-day MA, VIX < 20-day MA, and Brain Market Sentiment (BMS) > 20-day MA.
Exit Conditions: Exit at the next day's market open.
Indicators Used: SPY price, VIX price, Brain Market Sentiment (BMS) indicator.
Risk Management Rules: Suggested as an overlay rather than a standalone system due to high transaction costs.
Market Regime Suitability: Partially; stays out of equities during high-risk/high-volatility regimes.
Volatility Considerations: Estimated volatility is 7.33%; the strategy aims for low-volatility times.
Full Notes: Transaction costs are a major factor. The strategy is best used to decide when to hold existing equity positions overnight.
Mathematical/Statistical Concepts: Moving averages, Sentiment analysis, Overnight return anomaly.
